pageTimings.text:
The magic seeds were gone.
The box that held them was open and empty.
Luna looked at the box for a long time.
"This is not good," she said.

Luna called everyone together.
"The seeds keep Moonwood glowing," she said.
"Without them, the forest will grow dark."
Everyone looked at each other.

They split into groups.
Each group took a different path.
Each path led somewhere different.
"Find the seeds," said Luna.

Pip searched the Fog Marsh.
He found no seeds.
He found mud.
Flint searched the Tumblerock Cliffs.
He found no seeds.
He found three new things to map.

Wren and Spark tried search spells.
The spells pointed at each other.
Stone looked under every log.
Gently.
There were no seeds under the logs.

Burrow went underground.
He searched every tunnel.
He found three interesting things.
He did not find any seeds.

Dewdrop searched the stream.
Fern walked slowly around the clearing.
She was not searching.
She was listening.

Fern stopped.
She knelt down.
There were small green shoots in the ground.
They glowed.
Just a little.
Just like the seeds.

"Oh," said Fern.
She remembered.
Two nights ago she had been in the clearing.
She had planted the seeds.
She had not meant to forget.

Everyone came back with nothing.
Then they saw Fern.
She was sitting in the middle of the clearing.
Small glowing seedlings were growing all around her.
"Oh," said everyone.

Luna bent down to look.
The seedlings were strong.
They were growing along the paths.
Around the Hollow Oak.
All through the clearing.
"Better than a box," said Luna.

That night, Moonwood glowed more than ever.
The seedlings lit the ground.
Everyone sat outside the Hollow Oak.
"I'm sorry I forgot," said Fern.
"Don't be," said Luna.
"They like it better here."
